skill.name: claude-cookbooks
skill.description: Reference cookbooks for building Claude API applications, including tool use, RAG, vision, agents, and integration patterns.
skill.summary: Use Claude API cookbook examples to design and implement AI application patterns.
skill.author: 2025Emma

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, environment, and filesystem indicators. Review shows most are documentation examples or reference links, with no evidence of prompt injection or malicious exfiltration. The main publish risk is a filesystem-capable memory helper script and user-facing setup commands, so the skill should be published with warnings.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem-Capable Memory Helper Script
medium_findings.description: The Python memory handler can create, edit, rename, and delete files under its configured memory directory. Path validation confines operations to /memories, but destructive operations still require user awareness.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are direct and include shutil.rmtree. Confidence is high because the code also contains explicit path confinement, reducing but not removing the operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Pipe-To-Shell Installation Command In Documentation
medium_findings.description: The contribution guide includes a curl command piped directly to sh for installing uv. This is not executed by the skill, but users who follow it execute remote installer code in their shell.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pipe-to-shell pattern is explicit. It is documentation rather than hidden behavior, so the risk is user-guidance risk rather than confirmed malicious execution.

low_findings.title: Documentation Shell Commands Are Static Examples
low_findings.description: Many external command detections are fenced Markdown examples for uv, pip, git, pre-commit, and notebook validation. They are not invoked by the skill automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands appear inside Markdown code fences and development instructions. I found no code path that automatically runs these commands.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Reference Links
low_findings.description: Network detections are mostly links to Anthropic documentation, GitHub cookbook pages, and integration resources. I found no evidence that the skill sends data to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are Markdown links or resource references. No HTTP client code or exfiltration path was found in the inspected files.

low_findings.title: API Key Mentions Are Placeholder Or Safe Environment Usage
low_findings.description: The skill mentions API keys in examples and recommends environment variables. The example key is a placeholder and no real secret was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines show placeholder or environment-variable based secret handling. I did not find a committed credential or code that transmits secrets elsewhere.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithms, but reviewed lines describe Claude capabilities, RAG, or API examples. No MD5, SHA1, or cryptographic implementation was found in the inspected content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted search found no weak cryptographic APIs in the skill files. The finding is retained as low severity because it came from static analysis, but evidence supports dismissal.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Installer Piped To Shell
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup documentation shows a remote install script piped to sh. Users should review installer scripts or use a package manager where possible.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command form is clearly present in documentation. It is dangerous if copied blindly, although the skill does not execute it.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Directory Deletion
dangerous_patterns.description: The memory helper uses recursive deletion for memory directories. The code validates paths, but recursive delete behavior can remove user-created memory data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The recursive delete calls are direct and confirmed. Path validation limits scope to the configured memory root.

content.value_statement: Developers need reliable examples for Claude API features and application patterns. This skill organizes cookbook guidance for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains basic Claude Messages API usage with Python examples.
Summarizes tool use and function calling patterns for Claude applications.
Guides users to RAG, summarization, classification, and text-to-SQL cookbook topics.
Covers multimodal examples for image analysis, OCR, charts, and forms.
References third-party integration examples for vector databases, web search, embeddings, and audio transcription.
Includes a Python memory tool handler example with scoped file operations.
limitations: It is a reference skill, not a full runnable cookbook repository.
Some linked notebook examples are external and may change over time.
API examples require a valid Anthropic API key and local dependency setup.
The included memory script should be reviewed before use with important data.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Claude Feature
use_cases.description: Find the right cookbook pattern before writing a Claude API integration.

use_cases.title: Plan an AI Product Workflow
use_cases.description: Compare tool use, RAG, vision, and agent patterns for a product requirement.

use_cases.title: Teach Claude API Concepts
use_cases.description: Use organized cookbook references to explain common implementation patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Starting Example
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Claude Cookbooks skill to recommend a simple starting example for building my first Claude API app.

prompt_templates.title: Choose an API Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Claude Cookbooks skill to compare tool use, RAG, and vision patterns for this feature: [describe feature].

prompt_templates.title: Design an Implementation Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Claude Cookbooks skill to create an implementation plan for a Claude-powered workflow with tools, error handling, and testing.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Claude Cookbooks skill to review my Claude application architecture and identify missing safeguards, evaluation steps, and optimization options.

output_examples.input: I need to add function calling to a support assistant.
output_examples.output: A concise plan that points to tool use examples, recommends clear tool schemas, and lists validation and error handling steps.

output_examples.input: I want Claude to answer questions from internal documents.
output_examples.output: A RAG-oriented checklist covering document preparation, retrieval, context assembly, evaluation, and relevant cookbook references.

output_examples.input: I need to analyze uploaded forms and charts.
output_examples.output: A multimodal workflow outline with image quality guidance, extraction prompts, post-processing checks, and vision cookbook references.

best_practices: Start with the reference file that matches the feature you are building.
Use environment variables for API keys and keep secrets out of notebooks.
Review copied commands and scripts before running them locally.
anti_patterns: Do not paste real API keys into examples or prompt text.
Do not run remote install commands without reviewing the source.
Do not treat cookbook snippets as production code without tests and error handling.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps you use Claude API examples for tools, RAG, vision, agents, summarization, classification, and integrations.

faq.question: Does it include runnable code?
faq.answer: It includes snippets and one memory helper script, but most content is reference documentation.

faq.question: Do I need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The Claude API examples require an Anthropic API key for real execution.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with warnings. The audit found no malicious behavior, but users should review shell commands and filesystem scripts.

faq.question: Can Codex use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: What should I review before using it?
faq.answer: Review any copied terminal commands, dependency setup steps, API key handling, and the memory tool script.
